title: github jcob-bd/notebooklm-mcp-cliを調べて、kernel/FUGUEに活用できるか検証せよ。/kernelで。
summary_text: - NotebookLM handoff is end-to-end deterministic: `route-task-handoff` → `resolve-orchestration-context` → `fugue-bridge-handoff`/`fugue-codex-implement` always preserves `content_hint_applied`, `content_action_hint`, `content_skill_hint`, and `content_reason` for both `notebooklm-visual-brief` and `notebooklm-slide-prep`. - Label behavior is specific and exclusive: exactly one NotebookLM action label is applied per intent (`content-action:notebooklm-visual-brief` or `content-action:notebooklm-slide-prep`), plus `content:notebooklm`, with stale content labels removed. - Preflight runtime gating is complete: `notebooklm-preflight-enrich.sh` cleanly distinguishes `verified` (runtime success), `planned` (contract-only), and `blocked` (runtime required/auth unavailable), with consistent outputs and non-zero exit on blocked-required paths. - Execution consumption is wired and enforced: `codex-execute-validate.sh` receives NotebookLM report/summary/status/receipt envs and uses them as required evidence context rather than optional hint text. - Test completion bar is green and regression-safe: route/resolve/bridge/preflight tests all pass, including positive + negative coverage for NotebookLM label specificity and `content_reason` passthrough across direct and workflow-dispatch paths.
